title: Integral numbers should not be shifted by zero or more than their number of bits-1
description: The shifting operators are used to do an arithmetic shift to the bits of an integral numeric value, either to the left or the right. var number = 14; // ...01110 (14) var left = number << 1; // ...11100 (28) var right = number >> 1; // ...00111 (7) Therefore, shifting an integral number by 0 is equivalent to doing nothing, since the bits do not move any positions to the left or the right. On the other hand, shifting an integral number by a value greater than their count of bits minus one (n_bits-1) is equivalent to shifting by the value modulo the bit count of the number (value % n_bits).
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant: int result = value << 0;  // Noncompliant: shifting by 0 does nothing
int result = value << 32; // Noncompliant: shifting 32-bit int by 32 is undefined (value % 32 = 0)
long result = value << 64; // Noncompliant: shifting 64-bit long by 64 is undefined
int result = value >> -1; // Noncompliant: negative shift count
compliant: int result = value << 3;  // OK: meaningful shift by 3
int result = value >> 15; // OK: shift by 15 (valid for 32-bit int)
int result = value << 31; // OK: shift by 31 (max valid for 32-bit int, n_bits - 1)
long result = value << 63; // OK: shift by 63 (max valid for 64-bit long)

logic.detection_conditions: Detects bit shift operations with invalid shift counts (0, >= bit width, or negative)
logic.triggering_conditions: (Shift by 0) OR (Shift by >= 32 for int) OR (Shift by >= 64 for long) OR (Negative shift count)
exclusion_conditions: Valid shift counts (1 to 31 for int, 1 to 63 for long)
logic.reasoning: This rule detects invalid bit shift operations. Pattern matches: (1) '<< 0' or '>> 0' (no-op), (2) '<< 32' or '>>' for 32-bit ints (undefined, wraps to 0), (3) '<< 64' for 64-bit longs (undefined), (4) negative shift counts. Excludes valid shifts (1-31 for int, 1-63 for long). AST-based approach examines Assignment nodes for shift operators with invalid operands.
